summary: The satisfies/graph joins key on the bare member name, not the compiled `kind:name` label, so a cross-kind name collision cross-attributes edges into wrong findings, not silence

edit.description: `SatisfiesRow.member` (drift.rs:2311) carries a bare id where `MentionRow` already carries the `kind:name` address (identity is a compiled label — pipeline.md, 'The lock'). Qualify the wire to `kind:name`; `derive_layout_rows` (has `host_address()` in hand) writes the qualified label for layout-sourced satisfies. A pre-fix lock's bare rows stay accepted where unambiguous; a cross-kind collision is the malformed lock the spec already names — refused loud, one re-emit heals (posture per the fork).
`satisfiesRows` (declarations.ts:314) pushes `{ member: member.name }` — the bare name; write the `${member.kind}:${member.name}` address the way `mentionRows` already does (337/344).

`resolve_kind_units`' `row.member == unit.id` loop (main.rs:1019/1022) runs per kind — the collision leak; fold on the qualified label so a same-named member of another kind no longer cross-attributes.

tests.asserts: two kinds sharing a member name, each with a qualified satisfies row binding only its own kind's member, produce no cross-attributed requirement.kind refusals or false zero-degree findings
satisfies rows carry the `kind:name` label, not the bare member name

acceptance: A member name shared across two kinds no longer cross-attributes satisfies/graph edges; each qualified row binds only its own kind's member; a pre-fix lock's bare rows are accepted where unambiguous and a cross-kind collision is refused as a malformed lock.
notes: Live repro (centercode 07-15): a dev-pack `csharp` beside a rule `csharp` cross-attributed both satisfies + mention edges — false requirement.kind refusals both ways, false zero-degree, clean bisect on rename. Migration (spec-faithful, contra PR #20's keep-the-collision) is the posture the fork rules — hence dependsOnForks. Also moves tests/common/mod.rs author_satisfies, tests/emit.rs. observed 0aa9e62, wire re-verified f67303c; scoped at 06c44b1.

summary: A post-e7b859a engine's first emit against an older `./`-spelled lock silently mass-reaps every live projection — the orphan sweep matches no row and reaps all

edit.description: The orphan sweep (drift.rs:865-872) builds `owned_paths` via `to_lock_path` (drift.rs:501, backslash-only normalization) but compares against `read_prior_provenance` rows' raw `source_path`; an older lock spells them `./CLAUDE.md` while the post-e7b859a fingerprint pass keys the normalized `CLAUDE.md`, so every live projection reads ownerless and is reaped. Canonicalize the row side of the join too (strip `./`, the backslash handling as precedent) so a byte-identical projection matches its row — OR, per the fork, refuse the all-rows reap wave. Direction is the fork's to rule.

tests.asserts: an emit against a lock whose provenance rows carry `./`-prefixed source paths matches them to the live projections and reaps nothing (0 reaped), rather than mass-reaping every projection

acceptance: An emit under a normalized-path engine against an older `./`-spelled lock matches each byte-identical projection to its row and reaps nothing — no silent mass-reap; the loud-or-nothing invariant holds.
notes: Field incident (centercode 07-15): one emit reported '21 reaped', exit 0 — deleted every projection while writing a lock claiming them live and unchanged. e7b859a normalizes the workspace path pre-`harness_root`; to_lock_path (drift.rs:501) strips backslashes, never `./`. Direction (canonicalize the join key vs refuse the wave) is the fork's — hence dependsOnForks; serialized after SATISFIES (shared drift.rs). observed 0aa9e62, re-verified f67303c; scoped at 06c44b1.

summary: `emit --into <dir>` inside an adopted harness re-roots the projection tree, orphaning live projections the byte-faithful reap then deletes — emit must refuse the reap wave

edit.description: In the emit reap path (`reap_or_report_orphan` / `emit_program`), guard the mass-reap an `--into` re-root triggers: when the target re-roots an already-adopted harness, every prior projection reads as ownerless. Refuse (or dry-run-report) the whole wave instead of deleting live projections byte-faithfully.

The `--into` argument (Emit command: `into: PathBuf` main.rs:134, emit handler main.rs:308-316): carry whatever signal the reap guard needs to detect a re-root of an adopted harness (e.g. an existing lock at the ambient root the `--into` diverges from). Shares the arg/workspace resolution CHECK-ARG-HALF-GATE reworked (shipped 4256274).

tests.asserts: `emit --into <nested-dir>` inside an adopted harness refuses (or dry-run-reports) rather than reaping the repo-root projections; plain `emit` still reaps a genuinely single ownerless projection

acceptance: An `--into` that re-roots an already-adopted harness no longer silently deletes live projections — emit refuses or dry-run-reports the reap wave — while a genuine single-orphan reap still works.
notes: Observed at aba7e47: `emit --into .temper` reaped 7 live root projections (CLAUDE.md, five rules, a skill); restored via git, plain `emit` then clean 0/8/0. The prior reap fix (e7b859a) covered path spelling, not re-rooting. Loud-or-nothing (intent 6/7): emit never silently deletes. CHECK-ARG shipped 4256274 → open; reap-wave posture is the lock-upgrade fork's — still dependsOnForks. scoped at 01625fb.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary↔npm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) — no signing creds — but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Supersedes retired PACKAGING-CHANNELS (John, 07-11): both its file claims were dead — release.yml now exists (its `new` claim fails the refs gate) and the root-package.json launcher was ruled out (the launcher + optionalDependencies live on the SDK, distribution.md 'What ships'). Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. scoped at b676656.
